name: Ocean Pointe
desc: Newer oceanfront subdivision in Ewa — clean streets, community parks, young families, modern homes near the shore.
insider: The community has direct access to shoreline walking paths along the Ewa coast — great for morning jogs with ocean views
Homes here are newer construction (mostly 2000s-2010s) with modern open-concept layouts that are rare for Oahu's older housing stock
It's one of the few Ewa neighborhoods where you can actually see and smell the ocean from your street
The community pool and recreation center are well-maintained and serve as the main gathering spot for families
The shoreline is mostly rocky coral flats — not ideal for swimming but you'll spot sea turtles and monk seals regularly
HOA is around $100-150/month and covers the pool, common areas, and those manicured entrance landscaping that make it look so nice
Iroquois Point neighbors get military housing vibes — Ocean Pointe is the civilian version with similar proximity to the water
There's a nice keiki playground at Ocean Pointe Park that's shaded in the afternoon — smart design for Hawaii's sun
The nearest real grocery store is the Safeway on Fort Weaver Road — about a 5-minute drive, nothing walkable
Sunset views from the shoreline path are legitimately stunning — the Waianae Mountains silhouetted against orange sky is peak Hawaii
